plain_definition: A coordinated exercise in which ESMA and the national competent authorities (NCAs) of EU member states jointly examine a specific supervisory theme across a sample of regulated firms during the same period, using a shared methodology so results are comparable across countries. A CSA does not itself create new legal obligations for firms -- it is a supervisory-convergence tool: NCAs carry out the actual examinations of firms under their own national authorisation, and their findings are pooled into a consolidated report for ESMA's Board of Supervisors. ESMA's CSA on crypto-asset service providers (CASPs), announced 8 July 2026, focuses on the digital operational resilience of CASPs' custody activities, running from the second half of 2026 to the first half of 2027.
formal_definition: A supervisory-convergence tool used by ESMA under its Regulation (EU) No 1095/2010 mandate, through which ESMA coordinates a common examination programme that national competent authorities carry out, on a risk-based sample of supervised firms, against a shared set of criteria, with results consolidated into a report to ESMA's Board of Supervisors.
